title: narcissus romieuxii subsp. albidus
intro: Narcissus romieuxii subsp. albidus, also known as the white narcissus, is a rare and lesser-known species of the Narcissus genus, a type of flowering bulbous plant native to the mountains of southern France. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Narcissus romieuxii subsp. albidus are thought to include the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions or decoctions, and its extracts are sometimes used in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
